behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing, producing a clear error listing all missing items
behaviors.justification: This is pure input validation logic with no external dependencies or I/O. A unit test can fully exercise the validation function by passing payloads with missing fields and verifying correct error messages. Mocking downstream services does not hide validation bugs—the logic is entirely local.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test calling validation function with payloads missing one field, multiple fields, and all fields present. Verify error messages correctly list exactly which fields are missing.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings
behaviors.justification: Boundary validation with no I/O or external dependencies. Unit tests can exercise all boundary conditions (future date, date >150 years old, valid date in range, malformed strings) without needing any infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Parametrized unit test with cases: tomorrow's date (future), 200 years ago (too old), 50 years ago (valid), today (valid), '2024-13-45' (invalid format), 'not a date' (invalid).

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code to payer API and correctly interprets 'eligible' vs 'not eligible' vs 'pending' response
behaviors.justification: This involves an external API. While request construction could be unit-tested with mocks, correctly interpreting three distinct response types requires understanding the real payer API contract. Mocking assumed response formats (e.g., assuming a field called 'status') could hide failures when the real API uses different field names or structures. Integration testing with fixtures matching the real API contract catches both request errors and response-parsing bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with mocked HTTP responses matching real payer API contract. Test all three response types (eligible, not-eligible, pending) with realistic response bodies. Include edge cases: missing fields, unexpected fields, different field names, null values.

behaviors.description: When payer API is unreachable, system marks eligibility as 'pending' and continues intake rather than blocking
behaviors.justification: The behavior is about resilience under actual network failure, not just exception handling logic. A unit test mocking a generic exception doesn't test real timeout handling, connection refused errors, or DNS failures. Integration testing with realistic network error simulation (timeout exceptions, connection errors) ensures the fallback logic actually works when external services are unavailable.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that simulates payer API being unreachable by raising a timeout exception or connection error. Verify eligibility is marked as 'pending', and verify the intake process continues to the next step (symptom triage) without user intervention.

behaviors.description: AI symptom triage correctly assigns high urgency to symptom combinations indicating emergency conditions (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath)
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends entirely on what a real medical AI model produces. Different AI models or model versions will classify the same symptoms differently. Mocking the triage output (e.g., 'always return high urgency for chest pain') would hide the real risk: the deployed model may not correctly identify emergency conditions. This is not a code-logic bug; it is model behavior. Must test with the actual AI system.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic test calling the real symptom triage model with multiple emergency symptom combinations (chest pain + SOB, severe allergic reaction, loss of consciousness, serious bleeding). Verify that high urgency is returned for each. Repeat test multiple times to check consistency.

behaviors.description: Symptom triage produces natural language explanation of urgency assessment that a clinician can review, not just a numeric score
behaviors.justification: The output quality and format depend on what the real AI model generates. Mocking a template explanation would hide if the real model produces confusing, irrelevant, or unusable text. Clinician-reviewable quality is a model property, not code logic. Must test with the actual AI to verify explanations are coherent and clinically relevant.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic test calling real symptom triage with diverse symptom inputs. Verify output includes a natural language explanation field (not just a numeric urgency score). Spot-check explanations for coherence and clinical relevance (not templates, not gibberish).

behaviors.description: Appointment is scheduled in the first available slot matching the assigned urgency level (high urgency = same-day, medium = within 48 hours)
behaviors.justification: While the slot-selection logic could be unit-tested with mocked available slots, the behavior depends on correct database queries and transaction semantics. Mocking the slot list hides bugs in the actual query (e.g., wrong date filtering, missing status checks, ignoring timezone). Integration testing with a real test database ensures the system correctly queries and selects slots.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with test calendar database pre-populated with slots of different urgencies and times. Submit high-urgency intake and verify same-day slot is assigned. Submit medium-urgency and verify within-48-hour slot. Test edge case: insufficient same-day slots (verify fallback behavior).

behaviors.description: Appointment scheduling does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are being processed concurrently
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a concurrency race condition. Unit tests cannot exercise concurrent database access. Mocking the database away entirely hides the real failure: two transactions could select the same slot if not properly serialized. Integration testing with a real test database and concurrent test threads (or async execution) verifies locks and transactions prevent double-booking.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test launching two concurrent patient intake processes with the same urgency level, both selecting from the same pool of available slots. Verify that only one patient receives the contested slot and the other receives a different slot or appropriate error. Run multiple times to increase likelihood of catching timing bugs.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to the visit type
behaviors.justification: While message template formatting could be unit-tested with mocked data, ensuring all required fields are correctly sourced and populated requires verifying data consistency across multiple tables (patient, appointment, provider, visit_type). Mocking each source independently hides bugs like visit-type mismatches or missing provider data. Integration testing with data from multiple test tables ensures the confirmation correctly aggregates information.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test creating a complete patient intake with known values in test database (specific patient, provider, visit type). Retrieve confirmation message and verify it includes all required fields with correct values. Test multiple visit types to ensure instructions are appropriate (e.g., pre-appointment fasting for certain procedures).

behaviors.description: Full intake pipeline (validate → insurance check → symptom triage → schedule → confirm) completes successfully for a real patient submission without manual intervention
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end pipeline orchestrating five distinct steps: local validation logic, external payer API call, real AI symptom triage (agentic), database scheduling, and message generation. The pipeline includes an agentic step (AI triage) and requires correct sequencing and data flow across multiple subsystems. Mocking individual steps (e.g., mocking the AI) would hide integration failures like data format mismatches between steps, missing error handling between stages, or state loss. Workflow-level testing verifies the full orchestration works.
behaviors.test_description: Workflow test submitting complete patient intake with realistic data. Allow system to process through all five stages with real external calls (payer API, AI triage) or realistic integration mocks. Verify final confirmation is generated and contains expected data from all stages. Test with multiple patient profiles to verify pipeline robustness.
